refinement_queries: writable tmpfs mount docker run argv
shell wrap command execution stdin script
ephemeral copy working tree read-only snapshot
docker backend config default size limits
queries: docker sandbox writable overlay mount work directory
read-only work directory EROFS snapshot mount

build_from.reason: dockerRunArgs (line 110) builds the docker run argv including the current `-v spec.SnapshotDir:/work:ro` mount (line 140) and the global `--read-only` rootfs flag (line 117); this is the exact function the epic's Task 2/3 must modify. Run (line 161) is the only place spec.Command (Command mode, no shell) vs spec.Script (Script mode, piped to `sh -s` over stdin) diverge, confirming Command mode has no shell to interpret a prepended `cp` step.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add RunSpec.Writable (sandbox.go) and DockerConfig.WorkSize (docker.go), then branch dockerRunArgs's mount block and argv/stdin construction on spec.Writable: writable mounts SnapshotDir at /src:ro plus a new --tmpfs /work:rw,exec,size=<WorkSize>, and wraps Command mode in an explicit `/bin/sh -c '...' -- <argv>` shell while Script mode prepends the copy step to the script body before it is written to cmd.Stdin.

semantic_matches.query: docker sandbox writable overlay mount work directory
semantic_matches.relevance: Builds the docker run argv; owns the -v SnapshotDir:/work:ro mount, the --read-only rootfs flag, and the Command-vs-Script branch that appends the final payload to argv or switches to `/bin/sh -s`.

Calls dockerRunArgs then, for Script mode only, sets cmd.Stdin = strings.NewReader(spec.Script); Command mode's argv already contains the full exec, confirming there is no shell to inject a setup step into without wrapping.

Display-only human-readable command string for evidence/logging; confirmed to have zero effect on actual execution (res.Command), so setup-injection must NOT be placed here (this was a corrected assumption from the epic's first /refine-epic pass).

Target for the new Writable bool field; validate() (line 43) already enforces the exactly-one-of Command/Script invariant and absolute-path/no-colon SnapshotDir checks that any new field must not disturb.

semantic_matches.query: read-only work directory EROFS snapshot mount
semantic_matches.relevance: Package doc states as a hard MUST for every Backend.Run: 'a read-only view of the snapshot (the run cannot mutate the work tree)'. The opt-in design must keep this true for Writable:false (the default, used by --exec) and narrow the exception to the ephemeral /work tmpfs copy only when Writable:true.

The --auto-fix validation entry point; constructs sandbox.RunSpec{Command: argv, Timeout: timeout, SnapshotDir: dir} exclusively in Command mode (never Script) — this is where Writable: true must be set (Task 4).

Builds and preflights the sandbox.Backend used by --auto-fix; its doc comment (lines 47-55) duplicates the stale 'read-only /work limitation (effectively Go-only today)' note that docs/auto-fix.md also carries — both need updating per Task 6.
Sets ScratchSize: "64m" as the pattern to mirror for the new WorkSize field, sized larger since it holds a full source-tree copy rather than a build cache.

The two --exec call sites (Epic 11.0) that construct sandbox.RunSpec{...} without ever setting a Writable field today, confirming they will silently keep Writable:false (zero value) once the field is added, per the epic's opt-in design.

semantic_matches.query: read-only work directory EROFS snapshot mount
semantic_matches.relevance: Asserts joined argv contains "/tmp/snap:/work:ro" (line 55) — the exact contract that must remain byte-identical for Writable:false and is the regression anchor for Task 5(c).

Pre-existing test whose name coincidentally contains 'Writable' but tests the unrelated HOME/TMPDIR env-var redirection to /scratch — confirmed no naming collision with the new Writable field or its tests.

Asserts each RunSpec field RunSandboxedValidation forwards (Command, SnapshotDir, Timeout, and Script-empty at lines 68-71) via the recording fakeSandboxBackend — this is where Task 4's Writable: true must gain a pinning assertion. Surfaced by refinement gap analysis; the file was absent from the original discovery.

Recording docker-shim helper (:23) plus runArgsLine (:43) let resolver tests assert the exact `docker run` argv Preflight built from a SandboxConfig (:56). Because Preflight always runs with Writable:false, these tests are a control group proving Task 2's branch does not leak into default runs — and the natural pin-site if a SandboxConfig work_size knob is ever added.

semantic_matches.query: shell wrap command execution stdin script
semantic_matches.relevance: Pins the Script-mode argv shape (`-i <image> /bin/sh -s`) and that the script body never appears in argv — the pattern anchor for Task 5(a)'s Writable:true Script-mode assertions, where the prepended copy step must still travel over stdin, never interpolated into argv.

existing_patterns.name: Config-field-with-DefaultDockerConfig-mirror
existing_patterns.description: New tunables are added as a DockerConfig field with a sane constant default set inside DefaultDockerConfig() (e.g. ScratchSize: "64m"), never a package-level var.
existing_patterns.follow_for: WorkSize should follow the exact same pattern as ScratchSize.

existing_patterns.name: Opt-in-bool-RunSpec-field-defaulting-false
existing_patterns.description: RunSpec fields default to their Go zero value and every existing caller that does not set them gets today's behavior unchanged (e.g. Timeout: 0 defers to the backend default).
existing_patterns.follow_for: Writable bool should default to false and --exec's two call sites should require zero code changes.

existing_patterns.name: Pure-argv-builder-separate-from-I/O
existing_patterns.description: dockerRunArgs is deliberately pure (no I/O) so hardening flags can be asserted in a unit test without a daemon; Run does the actual exec.CommandContext spawn.
existing_patterns.follow_for: The Writable-mode mount/argv changes belong inside dockerRunArgs (pure, testable); only the Command-mode shell-wrap and Script-mode stdin-prepend touch spec.Command/spec.Script content, which also flows through dockerRunArgs's pure construction before Run consumes it.

existing_patterns.name: Fake-docker-shim-test-pattern
existing_patterns.description: Tests exercise Run/Preflight without a daemon via a POSIX shell shim impersonating the docker CLI: writeFakeDocker (internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go:24) writes an env-var-driven shim into t.TempDir(); fakeDockerRecording (internal/verify/autofix_exec_test.go:23) is the recording variant that appends every invocation's argv to a capture file. Both skip on Windows.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Task 5(b)'s proof that a mock validation script can write under /work when Writable:true should reuse writeFakeDocker with a body that simulates the mount layout (e.g. checks the argv carries the /work tmpfs and the cp setup, or emulates a writable /work), not a real daemon.

related_files.purpose: DockerConfig, DefaultDockerConfig, dockerRunArgs, renderCommand, DockerBackend.Run — the entire mount/argv/exec construction this epic modifies.
RunSpec struct, validate(), Backend interface, package doc pinning the read-only guarantee.
RunSandboxedValidation — the --auto-fix validation entry point that must opt into Writable: true.

fakeSandboxBackend records the RunSpec it receives; TestRunSandboxedValidation_RoutesThroughBackendWithBuiltSpec (:58) pins each forwarded field (Command/SnapshotDir/Timeout/Script-empty at :68-71) — must gain a Writable:true assertion so Task 4 is pinned by a test, not just code.
ResolveAutoFixSandbox builds/preflights the backend --auto-fix uses; its doc comment duplicates the stale EROFS limitation note.

Home of fakeDockerRecording (:23) and the resolver tests (TestResolveAutoFixSandbox_BuildsAndPreflights, :56) that assert the exact `docker run` argv Preflight builds from a SandboxConfig. Preflight always runs Writable:false, so these are control-group proof Task 2's branch does not alter default runs; also the pin-site if a SandboxConfig work_size field is ever mapped.
The --exec call sites (runTestsHandler, runScriptHandler via runInSandbox) that construct RunSpec without Writable — must remain behaviorally unchanged.

Handler-level tests asserting the RunSpecs the two --exec call sites build (e.g. TestRunScript_Handler_PassesContentAndTimeout, :148); the Writable zero value keeps them green untouched, and they are the optional site for an explicit Writable:false assertion on the --exec control group.

TestDockerRunArgs_HardeningFlagsPresent pins the current /work:ro mount contract; TestDockerRunArgs_ScriptUsesStdinShell (:83) pins the Script-mode argv shape; writeFakeDocker (:24) is the package's docker-CLI shim helper; TestDockerRunArgs_WritableTempEnv is an unrelated pre-existing test (name collision checked, none found).

DockerBackend-level behavior tests (exit-code classification, preflight host-cap checks, timeout kill) built on the shared writeFakeDocker shim — verified split: the shim itself is defined in sandbox_test.go:24, this file consumes it.

"What runs in the sandbox" section (lines 38-53), the standalone "Limitation (read-only /work)" paragraph (lines 55-60), and the EROFS blockquote (lines 62-71) all document the current always-read-only-/work behavior that Task 6 must update.

Documents --exec's read-only /work guarantee (lines 56-57, 86, 89); referenced by the epic's refinement as a guarantee that must remain textually accurate since --exec's default behavior is untouched, but the epic's Components Touched list does not include it — flagged as an open question below, not auto-included.

integration_points.description: Single conditional branch point for both the mount-list construction (SnapshotDir target + optional /work tmpfs) and the argv/stdin setup-step wrapping, keyed on spec.Writable.
Sole production call site that must flip Writable: true for --auto-fix; --exec's call sites in internal/tools/exec_tools.go are the control group that must NOT change.

integration_gaps.gap: docs/auto-fix.md's standalone "Limitation (read-only /work)" paragraph (lines 55-60) is not covered by Task 6's cited ranges
integration_gaps.impact: T6 names the section lines 38-53 and the EROFS blockquote lines 62-70; the paragraph between them tells operators writes into the working tree 'cannot' succeed and to redirect to /scratch or skip sandboxed validation — equally stale once RunSandboxedValidation sets Writable:true, and easy to miss because it falls in the gap between the two cited ranges.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Extend T6's docs scope (and the files_to_modify entry for docs/auto-fix.md) to rewrite lines 55-60 alongside line 47 and the blockquote.

integration_gaps.gap: internal/verify/sandboxvalidate_test.go was absent from the discovery; Task 4 has no pinning test site listed
integration_gaps.impact: T4 is a one-line change (Writable: true) with no listed test asserting it; the existing per-field forwarding assertions (:68-71) would still pass if the line were dropped in a future refactor.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Add a Writable:true assertion to TestRunSandboxedValidation_RoutesThroughBackendWithBuiltSpec (file added to files_to_modify as scope: extend).

integration_gaps.gap: No operator-facing config surface exists for WorkSize (registry.SandboxConfig has no ScratchSize analog either)
integration_gaps.impact: The plan's risk mitigation says to 'document the config knob so operators with larger trees can raise it', but SandboxConfig (internal/registry/sandbox.go:20-38) maps only DockerPath/Image/Memory/CPUs/PidsLimit/TimeoutSecs — following the ScratchSize pattern strictly means WorkSize is a code-level default only, with no YAML knob to document.
integration_gaps.recommendation: Decide explicitly during design: accept a code-only default (consistent with ScratchSize, which is also not operator-configurable) or add a sandbox YAML field plus a mapping in ResolveAutoFixSandbox (pinned in autofix_exec_test.go). Either way, T6's docs text should state whichever knob exists.

integration_gaps.gap: Writable:true Command mode introduces a new implicit container-image requirement (/bin/sh and cp -a)
integration_gaps.impact: Today's Command mode execs the image's argv directly with no shell at all; the /bin/sh -c 'cp -a /src/. /work/ && cd /work && exec "$@"' wrap requires the operator's image to ship a POSIX shell and a cp supporting -a (busybox in the alpine:3.20 default, coreutils in golang:* — but NOT distroless/scratch images).
integration_gaps.recommendation: Note the image requirement in docs/auto-fix.md as part of T6 and in the WorkSize/Writable field doc comments; failure mode on a shell-less image is a container start/exec failure, which surfaces as a backend fault (StartError) — acceptable but should be diagnosable.

reusable_components.name: DefaultDockerConfig / ScratchSize pattern (internal/sandbox/docker.go:53)
reusable_components.usage_example: Add `WorkSize: "<default>"` alongside `ScratchSize: "64m"` inside the same struct literal in DefaultDockerConfig() (docker.go:53-66).

reusable_components.name: writeFakeDocker shim helper (internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go:24)
reusable_components.usage_example: writeFakeDocker(t, body) installs a POSIX docker-CLI shim; Task 5(b) reuses it with a body keyed on the Writable:true argv (presence of the /work tmpfs flag and the cp wrap) to emulate a writable /work without a daemon.

reusable_components.name: limitedWriter / truncate output handling (internal/sandbox/docker.go:318-339)
reusable_components.usage_example: Unrelated to this epic's mount/argv work; no change needed.

architecture_notes: dockerRunArgs is pure and unit-testable without a Docker daemon — the epic's new mount/argv logic should stay inside it rather than moving into Run, preserving that testability.

The container's rootfs already runs under a global --read-only flag; only --tmpfs mounts (e.g. /scratch today) are writable. A Writable:true /work must be added as a NEW --tmpfs mount — renaming the existing -v bind target alone leaves /work with no writable backing and would still EROFS.

Command mode currently execs the image's argv directly with no shell (`args = append(args, cfg.Image); args = append(args, spec.Command...)`), confirmed at internal/sandbox/docker.go:146-147 — any setup step ahead of the real command requires an explicit `/bin/sh -c '... && exec "$@"' -- <command...>` wrap for Command mode specifically; Script mode already goes through `/bin/sh -s` so prepending to the script body is sufficient there.

RunSpec.validate() (sandbox.go:43) is the single choke point enforcing the exactly-one-of Command/Script invariant and SnapshotDir safety checks (absolute path, no ':'); Writable is an independent field and does not interact with these checks, so validate() likely needs no change unless Writable:true should require a non-empty SnapshotDir stricter than today (it already does).

Preflight (internal/sandbox/docker.go:281-316) builds its trivial-run argv via dockerRunArgs with RunSpec{Command: []string{"true"}} — Writable stays at its zero value, so the preflight path needs no change and its existing tests (docker_test.go:55-118) plus the resolver argv tests (autofix_exec_test.go:56) form a control group proving the Writable:false argv is byte-identical after the branch lands.

The Writable:true Command-mode shell wrap adds an implicit requirement that the container image provide /bin/sh and a cp supporting -a (busybox in the alpine:3.20 default, coreutils in golang:*); a distroless operator image would fail at container start. Surface this in the Writable/WorkSize doc comments and docs/auto-fix.md (see integration_gaps).

ScratchSize has no registry.SandboxConfig counterpart (grep-verified: it appears only in internal/sandbox/docker.go); strictly mirroring the ScratchSize pattern means WorkSize is a code-level default with no operator YAML knob — a deliberate decision point recorded in integration_gaps, not an oversight to fix silently.

files_to_modify.reason: Add `Writable bool` to RunSpec, defaulting to false via Go zero value.
Add `WorkSize string` to DockerConfig + default in DefaultDockerConfig(); branch dockerRunArgs's mount block and Command/Script setup-injection on spec.Writable.
Set Writable: true on the RunSpec built for --auto-fix validation.

Extend TestRunSandboxedValidation_RoutesThroughBackendWithBuiltSpec (:58) with a Writable:true assertion on the recorded spec, pinning Task 4 alongside the existing Command/SnapshotDir/Timeout/Script-empty forwarding assertions.
Update ResolveAutoFixSandbox's doc comment to remove the stale 'Read-only /work limitation (effectively Go-only today)' note.

Add Writable:true mount/argv assertions and the Writable:false regression assertion (byte-identical to today); TestDockerRunArgs_HardeningFlagsPresent's /work:ro assertion is the anchor, TestDockerRunArgs_ScriptUsesStdinShell (:83) is the Script-mode pattern, and writeFakeDocker (:24) is the shim Task 5(b) reuses.
Add a fakeDocker-based test proving a mock validation script can write a file under /work when Writable:true, for both Command and Script modes.

Update the unconditional 'mount mode is still read-only' claim (line 47), the standalone 'Limitation (read-only /work)' paragraph (lines 55-60 — sits between T6's cited ranges and is equally stale), and the EROFS blockquote (lines 62-71) to describe the opt-in writable overlay; also note the new implicit /bin/sh + cp -a image requirement for Writable Command mode.

test_patterns.example_test: internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go:35 (TestDockerRunArgs_HardeningFlagsPresent); shim helper writeFakeDocker at internal/sandbox/sandbox_test.go:24
